frosh-tools.title: Tools

index.title: System-Status
index.subtitle: Live health and performance checks for this Shopware instance.

cache.title: Cache
cache.subtitle: Manage cache pools, theme compilation and PHP opcache.

scheduledTaskOverview.title: Scheduled Tasks
scheduledTaskOverview.subtitle: Inspect, schedule, deactivate and run background tasks.

queue.title: Queue
queue.subtitle: Pending messages waiting to be processed.

purgeModal.title: Purge "{name}"
purgeModal.description: All messages in the transport "{name}" will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.

browse.title: Messages in "{name}"
browse.hint: Messages stay in the queue and are not consumed.
browse.hintRequeue: Messages are fetched and requeued: they get marked as redelivered, ordering may change and active workers compete for them.
browse.retrySuccess: The message has been dispatched to "{transport}" again

modal.title: Reset Queue
modal.description: Resetting Queue will remove all pending tasks.

logs.title: Log Viewer
logs.subtitle: Stream and inspect entries from any log file on disk.
logs.selectFileHint: Choose a file above to view its entries.

elasticsearch.title: Search
elasticsearch.subtitle: Cluster status, indices and a low-level query console.

tooltip.cleanup: Deletes indices reported by Shopware's outdated index detector.
tooltip.orphaned: Finds alias-less Shopware-prefixed indices that are not tracked by indexing tasks.

orphanedModal.title: Delete orphaned indices
orphanedModal.description: This heuristic cleanup deletes alias-less Shopware-prefixed indices. Indices that are still tracked by storefront or admin indexing tasks are excluded and the server revalidates this list before deletion.

deleteIndex.title: Delete index "{name}"?
deleteIndex.description: The index "{name}" and all its documents will be permanently deleted. Storefront search may be empty until a reindex finishes.

flushAll.title: Delete all indices?
flushAll.description: All indices, including the active ones, will be permanently deleted. Storefront search will be empty until a reindex finishes.

reset.title: Reset Search?
reset.description: All indices will be deleted and the index aliases will be recreated from scratch. Storefront search will be empty until a reindex finishes.

cleanup.partialError: {count} error(s) occurred while deleting. Please check the logs.

feature-flags.title: Feature Flags
feature-flags.subtitle: Feature flags exposed by Shopware core and registered extensions.

files.title: Shopware File Checker
files.expectedProject: File has been modified, but will be ignored by project settings

extensionFiles.title: Extension File Checker
extensionFiles.notOk: There are errors or modified extensions. JS, CSS, or snippet files may have been modified by deployment scripts without this posing a problem.
extensionFiles.wrongExtensionVersion: The checksum file was created for a different extension version.
extensionFiles.checkFailed: The extension could not be checked. Please check the logs.

security.title: Security Center
security.subtitle: Continuous security overview: dependency advisories, runtime end-of-life, and risky configuration.
security.notice: Security matters for every publicly reachable shop — not only production. Staging, preview, and test environments are frequently exposed and targeted, so run these checks there too.

verdict.unknown: Some checks could not be completed

composerAudit.title: Composer Audit
composerAudit.subtitle: Check installed Composer packages against the Packagist security advisories database.

explainer.title: What is this?
explainer.what: Your shop is built from many smaller software building blocks (called "dependencies") — both Shopware itself and the plugins and libraries it uses. This page checks the exact versions installed in your shop against a public database of known security problems.
explainer.why: When a problem (an "advisory") is found, it means a building block your shop relies on has a known weakness that attackers could potentially exploit. The good news: almost all of them are already fixed in a newer version — your shop just needs to be updated to it.
explainer.who: Updating these building blocks is a technical task. If you don't manage your shop's code yourself, send this list (or a screenshot) to your developer or agency and ask them to apply the updates — the step-by-step instructions below are written for them.

composerAudit.cachedAt: Cached at {at}. Click Refresh for a fresh check.
composerAudit.scannedPackages: {count} installed packages checked against Packagist advisories.
composerAudit.vulnerableBody: {vulnerable} of {total} installed packages have security advisories.

howto.title: How to fix these advisories
howto.backup: Always create a full backup (database and files) before updating dependencies, so you can roll back if an update breaks your shop.
howto.step1: On your local or CI environment, update the affected packages with Composer. The command below names exactly the packages with advisories; "--with-dependencies" lets Composer also update their required sub-dependencies when a patched version needs it, while leaving the rest of your project untouched:
howto.step2: Review the changes (especially major version bumps) and run your test suite to catch regressions.
howto.step4: Commit the updated composer.lock and deploy. The check above re-runs automatically (cached up to 1 hour — use Refresh to force it).
howto.note: Some advisories are fixed only in a newer major version. If a constraint in your composer.json blocks the update, you may need to raise it (e.g. "^2.0") before Composer can install the patched release.

state-machines.title: State Machine Viewer
state-machines.subtitle: Visualize states, transitions and actions for any registered state machine.
state-machines.noSelectionHint: Pick one from the dropdown above to render its diagram.
state-machines.label: State Machine

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.subtitle: Performance metrics for cache backends and the database.
statistics.loadError: Could not load statistics. Check that the API is reachable and you have the required permissions.

fastly.title: Fastly
fastly.description: Here you can clear the Fastly cache. You can either clear the entire cache or just a specific URL (path).
fastly.purgeAllError: Could not clear complete fastly cache

hero.description: Monitor all your Shopware environments from one central dashboard. Shop status, performance metrics, extension updates, and security alerts - all in real-time without switching between tools.
hero.hint: This creates a restricted API integration in this shop that Shopmon uses to read its status.

status.title: Shop status
status.body: Health checks, Shopware version, queue and scheduled task status of all your environments at a glance.

updates.title: Extension updates
updates.body: See pending Shopware and extension updates across all shops and track every change over time.

alerts.title: Security alerts
alerts.body: Get notified about known security issues in your installed Shopware version and extensions.

setup.success: The Shopmon integration has been created
setup.error: The Shopmon integration could not be created

configured.subtitle: This shop is ready to be monitored with Shopmon.
configured.readyBody: An API integration for Shopmon has been created. Use the quick setup code below to add this shop in Shopmon.
configured.setupCodeHint: Paste this code in Shopmon when adding a new shop — it contains the shop URL and the API credentials.
configured.step1: Open shopmon.fos.gg and sign in or create a free account.
configured.step2: Add a new shop in your organization and paste the quick setup code shown above.
configured.step3: Shopmon will connect to this shop and start monitoring it right away.

remove.success: The Shopmon integration has been removed
remove.error: The Shopmon integration could not be removed

modal.title: Remove Shopmon integration
modal.description: This deletes the API integration used by Shopmon. Shopmon will no longer be able to monitor this shop until a new integration is created.

frosh-tools.name: Name
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskStarted: The scheduled task execution for "{name}" started
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskSucceed: The scheduled task execution for "{name}" succeed
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskFailed: The scheduled task execution for "{name}" failed
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskScheduleStarted: The scheduled task for "{name}" is being scheduled
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskScheduleSucceed: The scheduled task for "{name}" has been scheduled
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskScheduleFailed: The scheduled task for "{name}" could not be scheduled
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskDeactivateStarted: The scheduled task for "{name}" is being deactivated
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskDeactivateSucceed: The scheduled task for "{name}" has been deactivated
frosh-tools.scheduledTaskDeactivateFailed: The scheduled task for "{name}" could not be deactivated
